message:
fix(remote,net,plan,hedge): extract request_cancel helper, remaining unwrap fixes, hedge test fix

Remote (remote.rs):
- Extracted request_cancel() helper that wraps abort_with() with the
  handle's own origin_node and sender_clock. Drop impl, close(), and
  abort() all now delegate to this single entry point, eliminating
  three separate copies of the same origin_node.clone() + tick() pattern.
- close() now reads cancel_reason from Cx directly and passes it to
  request_cancel instead of going through abort() which would re-read it.
- New test: remote_handle_close_with_plain_context_still_requests_cancel_when_runtime_attached
  verifies that close() works even with a Cx that has no remote cap,
  using the handle's retained origin and clock.

Net split (tcp/split.rs, unix/split.rs):
- One remaining unwrap() on combined_waker converted to expect().

Plan fixtures (plan/fixtures.rs):
- SharedLabHandle::join unwrap() converted to expect().

Hedge service (service/hedge.rs):
- Test assertion changed from assert!(is_pending()) to match with a
  panic message showing the actual result on failure, improving
  debuggability when the hedge timer fires unexpectedly early.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -660,7 +660,7 @@ impl SharedLabHandle {
             let result = guard
                 .handle
                 .as_mut()
-                .unwrap()
+                .expect("handle must exist")
                 .join(&cx)
                 .await
                 .unwrap_or_default();
